summary: TYS Fase 1 end-to-end testing. Backend 41/41 PASS (100%). Frontend critical flows verified: landing+plans, admin login redirect, admin organizer list with 5 rows (3 seeded demos + 2 created during tests), pending organizer dashboard, onboarding wizard, admin planes with 4 plans & active switches. Stripe checkout-session works with sk_test_emergent for BOTH mode=subscription and mode=payment (confirmed via backend logs). Webhook simulator (subscription.deleted + subscription.updated) idempotent. All RBAC rules enforced (401/403). Seed idempotent (4 plans, 3 demo orgs, 1 super_admin). Two frontend issues are SELECTOR-LEVEL only: 'Ver' detail link missing expected data-testid prefix, and Register form inputs not using data-testid attributes I attempted.

issues: Form fields (email/password/company_name/legal_id/phone/country) appear to lack data-testid attributes (register-email, register-password, etc.). Required for robust E2E. Form itself renders and HTML5 validation triggers correctly.
The 'Ver' (detail) link in each row may be missing a data-testid like 'organizer-view-{id}'. UI works (5 rows visible with Ver buttons) but automated selectors couldn't pick it up.

action_items: Add data-testid attributes on Register form inputs: register-email, register-password, register-company-name, register-legal-id, register-phone, register-country, register-org-type-company/individual, register-submit.
Add data-testid on admin organizer table action: e.g. `data-testid="organizer-view-{id}"` on each row 'Ver' link to enable robust E2E selectors.
(Nice-to-have) Migrate FastAPI @app.on_event to lifespan context manager (deprecation warning).

(Nice-to-have) Legacy POC page src/pages/Payments.jsx has a JSX parsing error in the eslint output. Doesn't break the build per latest logs but should be cleaned up since POC is out-of-scope for Fase 1.
critical_code_review_comments: Excellent: All endpoints under /api prefix; openapi spec confirmed under /api/openapi.json with zero non-/api paths.
Excellent: Cookies httpOnly tys_access + tys_refresh set on login, cleared on logout; refresh issues new access token; Bearer fallback also supported.
Excellent: Seed idempotent (verified: re-running tests doesn't duplicate; counts stable). MRR calc only counts monthly plans (correct).
Excellent: RBAC consistently returns 401 (no cookie) vs 403 (wrong role).
Excellent: register endpoint correctly returns 200 with user+organizer but does NOT set cookies (no auto-login as per spec).
Excellent: Stripe sk_test_emergent works for mode=subscription + mode=payment via integrations.emergentagent.com — no 502 observed in this run.
Note: GET /api/admin/organizers returns 5 rows after register tests (3 demos + 2 created by tests). Tests scope is fine since seed is idempotent; consider auto-cleanup of TEST_-prefixed orgs.
Note: /api/stripe/_simulate_webhook supports customer.subscription.updated with arbitrary status — undocumented but handy for restoring demo-org state after a canceled test.

success_rate.frontend: 71% (5/7 happy-path flows verified; remaining 2 are selector-only issues, UI itself works)

test_credentials: admin@ticketyourself.com/Admin123!, demo@ticketyourself.com/Organizer123! (approved), prueba@ticketyourself.com/Organizer123! (pending), rechazado@ticketyourself.com/Organizer123! (rejected). Also created 2 throwaway organizers during tests.
seed_data_creation: Test suite registers ephemeral organizers using new_*@example.com / acttest_*@example.com / slugtest_*@example.com / ui_test_*@example.com. Demo-org subscription was canceled then restored to active via simulator. No production data altered.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase1.py covers full Fase 1. Re-run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_phase1.py -v. If main agent adds data-testids on Register + admin organizer 'Ver' link, the two flagged frontend selector cases will pass. Note: tests insert temp organizers (new_/acttest_/slugtest_/ui_test_) — DB will grow but counts in stats test use >= so it stays stable. Stripe wrapper supports BOTH subscription and one_time modes in this environment, so previous worry about 502 on mode=subscription is moot.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. The two frontend 'failures' in this iteration are due to missing data-testid attributes on Register form inputs and the admin organizer 'Ver' detail link; the underlying UI works correctly (verified by screenshots showing 5 organizer rows with visible 'Ver' buttons, and the register form rendering correctly with browser-side validation).
